subject: Chemistry

learning_objectives.statement: Identify the solubility of a salt, and/or the value of Ksp for the salt, based on the concentration of a common ion already present in solution.

depth_profile: Students must understand that the solubility of a salt is reduced when it is dissolved into a solution that already contains one of the ions present in the salt. The impact of this 'common-ion effect' on solubility must be understood qualitatively using Le Châtelier's principle, and students must be able to calculate it quantitatively from the Ksp for the dissolution process.
assessment_notes: This topic is assessed alongside the science practice skill 2.F (Explain how modifications to an experimental procedure will alter results). Students may be asked to identify the solubility of a salt or the value of Ksp based on the concentration of a common ion already present in solution, and explain how the presence of that ion alters the experimental outcome.

spec_checklist.can_do: I can identify the solubility of a salt, and/or the value of Ksp for the salt, based on the concentration of a common ion already present in solution.

spec_source_citation: AP Chemistry Course and Exam Description, V.1
